none: Lime manufacturing plants standards : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed new standards to control particulate emissions from lime manufacturing plants. These standards aim to significantly reduce air pollution by requiring facilities to limit emissions from rotary lime kilns and hydrators. The proposal is part of the Clean Air Act's efforts to protect public health and welfare by implementing the best available technology for emission reduction. The standards are expected to have both environmental and economic impacts, including improved air quality and associated costs for the industry.
Lime manufacturing plants standards : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed new standards to control particulate emissions from lime manufacturing plants. These standards aim to significantly reduce air pollution by requiring facilities to limit emissions from rotary lime kilns and hydrators. The proposal is part of the Clean Air Act's efforts to protect public health and welfare by implementing the best available technology for emission reduction. The standards are expected to have both environmental and economic impacts, including improved air quality and associated costs for the industry.
Office of Air Quality Planning and Standards

The EPA proposed new standards for lime manufacturing plants to address air pollution concerns, particularly from particulate emissions. This initiative was part of broader efforts under the Clean Air Act to enhance public health and environmental quality. While there was some industry pushback regarding the economic implications of the new regulations, the overall goal was to implement the best available technology for emission reduction. The proposal aimed to balance environmental benefits with industry costs.

"The beneficial and adverse environmental impacts associated with the proposed standards and with the various emission control alternatives that were considered in selecting the standards are presented in this section." — Environmental Impact Section
